title: CTAB–Chloroform DNA Extraction from Ethanol-Preserved Freshwater Phytobentos
author: Pavla Urbankova

steps.title: Resuspend particulates in the ethanol-preserved sample...
steps.instruction: Resuspend particulates in the ethanol-preserved sample container by manual shaking until homogenized.

steps.title: Transfer 2 mL of homogenized ethanol mixture into a 2
steps.instruction: Transfer 2 mL of homogenized ethanol mixture into a 2.0 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 13500 rpm, Room temperature, 20 minutes (max speed)
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 13500 rpm, Room temperature, 20 minutes (max speed).

steps.title: Dry the pellet for 00:10:00 using...
steps.instruction: Dry the pellet for 00:10:00 using either a vacuum concentrator or a heat block (open cap), ensuring ethanol is removed.

steps.title: Carefully decant the supernatant without disturbing the pellet
steps.instruction: Carefully decant the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.

steps.title: Add 500 µL CTAB buffer (room...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL CTAB buffer (room temperature) to the dried pellet.

steps.title: Vortex continuously for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Vortex continuously for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Freeze at -20 °C until fully...
steps.instruction: Freeze at -20 °C until fully frozen (typically 01:00:00 ); samples may be stored at -20 °C Overnight and processed the next day.

steps.title: Thaw at 65 °C for 00:15:00
steps.instruction: Thaw at 65 °C for 00:15:00 .

steps.title: Add 4 µL 20 µL Proteinase...
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL 20 µL Proteinase K ( 20 mg/mL ) and vortex briefly.

steps.title: Incubate at 65 °C for 01:00:00
steps.instruction: Incubate at 65 °C for 01:00:00 .

steps.title: Add 400 µL chloroform and mix...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL chloroform and mix gently with the pipette tip.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 13500 rpm, Room temperature, 15 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 13500 rpm, Room temperature, 15 minutes .

steps.title: Carefully transfer ~ 300 µL of...
steps.instruction: Carefully transfer ~ 300 µL of the upper aqueous phase to a new 2.0 or 1,5mL tube using a fresh filter tip, avoiding the interphase.

steps.title: Add 400 µL ice-cold isopropanol (stored...
steps.instruction: Add 400 µL ice-cold isopropanol (stored at -20 °C ) and mix by inverting the tube several times.

steps.title: Incubate 00:15:00 at 4 °C
steps.instruction: Incubate 00:15:00 at 4 °C .

steps.title: Centrifuge for 13500 rpm, Room temperature, 15 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 13500 rpm, Room temperature, 15 minutes .

steps.title: Remove supernatant carefully without losing the pellet
steps.instruction: Remove supernatant carefully without losing the pellet.

steps.title: Add 500 µL ice-cold 70% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Add 500 µL ice-cold 70% ethanol (stored at -20 °C ) and briefly vortex.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 13500 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 13500 rpm, Room temperature, 5 minutes .

steps.title: Carefully remove supernatant
steps.instruction: Carefully remove supernatant.

steps.title: Repeat etanol wash once more go to step #19
steps.instruction: Repeat etanol wash once more go to step #19 .

steps.title: Dry the pellet (open cap) for...
steps.instruction: Dry the pellet (open cap) for ~ 00:10:00 in a vacuum centrifuge or on a 65 °C heat block (avoid airborne contamination).

steps.title: Dissolve DNA pellet in 30 µL...
steps.instruction: Dissolve DNA pellet in 30 µL EB buffer (or sterile milliQ water), vortex and spin down. Let the DNA dissolve for at least 1 hour before further analysis (or store in fridge/freeze).
